jade.name: Jade DIY
jade.summary: Open-source hardware wallet, secure and flexible.
jade.details_html:
Jade DIY Device Documentation

Introduction: secure and transparent device.

Why open-source hardware? Article

Connectivity: GreenWallet • SideSwap • Sparrow • Electrum

Updates: via browser / Sandmann binaries / GitHub build

Battery: internal (limited indicator)

Security: Oracle virtual secure-element

Tutorial: YouTube

Full documentation

pico.name: PicoFido / PicoKey (RP2040 • FIDO2)
pico.summary: FIDO-U2F / FIDO2 security key based on RP2040 (USB-C).
pico.details_html:
Technical Details

• 100% open-source project — code, schematics and guides at github.com/polhenarejos/pico-fido.

• Compatible: Google, Proton Mail, Microsoft Outlook, GitHub, X/Twitter…

• FIDO2 replaces SMS/app 2FA; keys stay on the device.

• Female USB-C; host-powered (cable not included).

• Connectivity: Android • Linux • Windows (iOS/macOS not tested).

• Full documentation.

nerd.name: NerdMiner — CASE (no battery)
nerd.summary: CASE for NerdMiner (TTGO T-Display). *Does not include battery or board.
nerd.details_html:
• ~55 KH/s (project info) • Stratum / self-custody pools.
• Firmware: GitHub.
• USB-C 5 V • Tutorial: YouTube.

TTGO T-Display: Buy on AliExpress

CASE ONLY — no battery.

sandseed.name: SandSeed – seed backup plates
sandseed.summary: Stakbit 1248 Kit – 5 plates (3 plain + 2 perforated) BIP-39 standard.
sandseed.details_html:
How to use: on a firm surface, punch each letter (cutter, thick needle or punch).

Purpose: physical, offline backup resistant to fire/water for your 24 BIP-39 seed words.

Tutorial: Official video (Stackbit)

Original design by @Valandro. Stainless steel version at author's store.
